ddls:
CREATE TABLE "service_registries" (
  "registry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service registry */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the registry center */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used by the registry */,
  "port" INTEGER /* Port of the service registry */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for validated access to the registry */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for validated access to the registry */,
  "registry_type" TEXT /* Type of the registry (e.g., Zookeeper, Consul, Nacos) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the registry entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the registry entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("registry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "registry_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each registry configuration */,
  "registry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service registry it belongs to */,
  "attribute" TEXT /* Name of the configuration attribute */,
  "url_param" TEXT /* Corresponding URL parameter for the attribute */,
  "type" TEXT /* Data type of the attribute */,
  "required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is required */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the attribute */,
  "function" TEXT /* Functionality description of the attribute */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the attribute */,
  "validation_regex" TEXT /* Validation regular expression for the attribute */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_registry_configurations_registry_id FOREIGN KEY ("registry_id") REFERENCES service_registries ("registry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "registry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service registry */,
  "can_edit" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user can edit the registry configuration */,
  "can_view" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user can view the registry configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_registry_id FOREIGN KEY ("registry_id") REFERENCES service_registries ("registry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_change_logs" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change log entry */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration that was changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., ADD, UPDATE, DELETE) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_change_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_change_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES registry_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "supported_protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol (e.g., Dubbo, Zookeeper) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol capabilities */,
  "documentation_url" TEXT /* URL to the protocol documentation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "default_values" (
  "value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each default value record */,
  "attribute" TEXT /* Attribute name for which the default value applies */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the attribute */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the default value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_default_values_attribute FOREIGN KEY ("attribute") REFERENCES registry_configurations ("attribute")
)

CREATE TABLE "compatibility" (
  "compatibility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compatibility record */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration */,
  "version" TEXT /* Minimum version required for compatibility */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the compatibility version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compatibility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES registry_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ci_cd_pipelines" (
  "pipeline_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pipeline */,
  "pipeline_name" TEXT /* Name of the pipeline */,
  "registry_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service registry */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the pipeline was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the pipeline was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pipeline_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ci_cd_pipelines_registry_id FOREIGN KEY ("registry_id") REFERENCES service_registries ("registry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pipeline_executions" (
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pipeline execution */,
  "pipeline_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the pipeline */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who triggered the pipeline */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the pipeline execution started */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the pipeline execution ended */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the pipeline execution (e.g., SUCCESS, FAILURE, IN_PROGRESS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pipeline_executions_pipeline_id FOREIGN KEY ("pipeline_id") REFERENCES ci_cd_pipelines ("pipeline_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pipeline_executions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
